Examined script improvements and debugging strategies

Creating the mirror directory structure for changed files

New module initializer that points Testcontainers at rootless Podman so the 40 integration tests stop skipping on the user's Fedora machine

Replacement PostgreSqlFixture with an actionable skip message for the rootless-Podman case

Replacement DapperSecurityEventLogTests fixing the xUnit1051 warning on line 90 (cancellation token now passed to the guard test)
Examined script improvements and debugging strategies.


Improved quick-tunnel helper: readiness pre-check plus a prominently surfaced trycloudflare URL, still foreground per §14.3

Syntax-checking the rewritten tunnel script

New claims principal factory: fixes the missing role claims (single-generic default factory never emits them) and adds obligation + display-name claims

New pure enforcement helpers for the obligations pipeline: routes, claim mapping, exempt paths, redirect targets — all unit-testable without a server

New obligations middleware enforcing §3.5: authenticated principals with an outstanding flag are redirected away from everything but the pipeline pages and sign-out

New minimal-API account endpoints: the antiforgery-protected POST /sign-out

Replacement identity wiring: registers the claims factory, cascading authentication state, and updates the documentation now that the sign-in surfaces exist

Replacement sign-in manager adding the deactivated-account gate (CanSignInAsync) now that real sign-in pages reach it

Replacement Program.cs: obligations middleware in the pipeline and the sign-out endpoint mapped

Replacement App.razor: per-page render mode so account pages can render static SSR (cookie writes) while everything else stays interactive server

Replacement Routes.razor: AuthorizeRouteView with sign-in redirect for anonymous users and an inline denial for authenticated-but-unauthorized ones

Replacement _Imports.razor with the authorization, identity, and account namespaces the new pages use

Replacement MainLayout with a session header: username plus sign-out for authenticated visitors, sign-in link otherwise; works in static SSR and interactive rendering
Done
Now the account pages themselves:


Created 10 files
Created 10 files

New RedirectToSignIn component used by the router for anonymous visitors hitting protected pages

New sign-in page (static SSR): username + password, routing to the TOTP challenge, lockout and deactivation messaging, open-redirect-safe return URL

New TOTP challenge page for the password path (static SSR)

New recovery-code sign-in page for the password path (static SSR)

New forced password-change page — obligation (1) of §3.5: verifies current password, sets the new one, clears the flag in the same update, records the event, refreshes the cookie

New forced TOTP-enrollment page — deliberately a stub until the TOTP enrollment slice lands; flag can only exist after the admin-reset slice anyway

New access-denied page matching the cookie's AccessDeniedPath

New first [Authorize]-gated area page: /table placeholder proving the whole auth chain end to end

Replacement Home.razor reflecting that sign-in now exists and linking the gated table area

Replacement app.css: everything from M1 plus the session header, account panels, and form styling the sign-in slice needs
Done

New unit tests for the claims factory using a hand-written fake store — guards the role-claims fix and the obligation claims

Apply notes for this delivery — grouped file list, one-time host fix, verification checklist

Bundle the slice as tar.gz and copy every file to outputs preserving repo-relative paths

Verify the tar bundle contains all 32 files and nothing stray
Orchestrated file organization and structured output presentation.


Presented 33 files
Synthesizing final answer regarding milestone requirements.

Synthesized technical findings and structured comprehensive explanation.

Done
All 32 files are above — full replacements with repo-relative paths, plus the tar.gz that unpacks the whole tree from your repo root. CHANGES-2026-07-19.md is the apply-and-verify guide.

On your terminal "errors": nothing failed. Build was green (one xUnit1051 warning, now fixed), 0 test failures, and --smoke passed. The ~40 skips were Testcontainers not finding a container socket. One-time fix: systemctl --user enable --now podman.socket — the new ContainerEngineDiscovery.cs then finds the rootless Podman socket automatically, and the fixture's skip message now tells you the exact command if it ever regresses. With the socket active, expect roughly 217 passed / 3 skipped.

On the Opus claim: it was correct. Spec §14.4 defines run.sh as compose-up-and-watch with only --smoke and --containers-only as modes, and §14.3 plus ADR-0005 confine quick tunnels to the demo-only scripts/quick_tunnel.sh. So no milestone delivers "run.sh prints a trycloudflare URL and exits" — and none could as written, because a quick tunnel dies with the cloudflared process that owns it; a URL printed by an exiting script is a dead URL. The supported demo is two commands: ./run.sh --containers-only, then scripts/quick_tunnel.sh (which now pre-checks readiness, prints the URL in a banner, and holds the foreground until Ctrl+C). If you want a one-command --demo mode, that's a spec change for you to rule on — it's a reasonable ask, just not currently in any milestone. OPERATIONS.md §10, README, and BUILD_PROGRESS all now record this so the question stays settled.

Milestone progress (M2 slice 3): the sign-in surface is now real — /sign-in, TOTP and recovery-code steps, forced-password-change and (stub) TOTP-enrollment obligation pages, all static SSR; an obligations middleware that fences authenticated-but-obligated users; antiforgery-validated sign-out; and /table as the first policy-gated interactive page. Along the way I fixed a latent bug worth knowing about: AddIdentityCore's default claims factory emits no role claims, so your area policies could never have passed — the new RestaurantClaimsPrincipalFactory fixes that, and CanSignInAsync now turns deactivated accounts away at sign-in. No new packages, no new migrations. Next slice is real TOTP enrollment to replace the stub, then passkeys and the /setup bootstrap.
